response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable polyglot engineer with a strong testing culture and some distributed systems exposure, but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience as a hard minimum — the candidate lists no C++ in his skills section and has only a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components plus a thesis project, amounting to well under a year of practical C++ work. His technical leadership experience is implicit rather than demonstrated, and while he has touched AWS CDK and Kubernetes, there is no evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform at any meaningful scale.
strengths: Strong distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-throughput micro-services
Deep testing and software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source testing libraries) spanning the entire career
Broad infrastructure breadth including Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, and DevOps tooling across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (approximately 6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis) versus the 8-year hard minimum required
No documented technical leadership role with explicit mentoring, architectural ownership, or cross-team alignment responsibilities at the scale the JD describes
Primary expertise is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang web and product engineering — no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, hardware architecture, or storage systems work
